title: gratiola pubescens
intro: Gratiola pubescens, also known as American balm or American bugleweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of Gratiola pubescens include its use as an antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant, making it effective in treating conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and gastrointestinal problems. Gratiola pubescens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
